study_guide_title: STA 111 Descriptive Statistics - Comprehensive Past Questions

questions.question: Statistics is primarily concerned with:
questions.explanation: Statistics is the science of collecting, analyzing, interpreting, presenting, and organizing data.

questions.question: Qualitative data refers to:

options.c: Data that can be measured exactly

questions.explanation: Qualitative data represents categories or labels (e.g., gender, blood type).

questions.question: Which of the following is a continuous variable?

options.a: Number of students in a class
options.d: Number of cars in a parking lot

questions.explanation: Continuous variables can take any value within a range (e.g., height, weight).

questions.question: Which of the following is a discrete variable?

options.b: Number of cars in a parking lot

questions.explanation: Discrete variables take countable values (0, 1, 2, ...).

questions.question: Which of the following is a quantitative variable?
questions.explanation: Quantitative variables are numerical and measurable (e.g., age, height, weight).

questions.question: Which of the following is a discrete variable?

options.a: Number of students in a class

questions.explanation: Discrete variables take countable values (0, 1, 2, ...). Number of students is countable.

questions.question: A pie chart is used to:

options.a: Show the relationship between two variables

questions.explanation: Pie charts display data as proportions of a whole, usually in percentages.

questions.question: A histogram is used to display:
questions.explanation: Histograms are for continuous or interval data, showing frequency distribution.

questions.question: A bar chart is mainly used to display:
questions.explanation: Bar charts represent frequencies of discrete/categorical variables.

questions.question: A histogram differs from a bar chart in that:

options.b: Bars touch each other in histogram

questions.explanation: Histogram bars touch each other to indicate continuous data intervals.

questions.question: Which type of graph is suitable for showing parts of a whole?
questions.explanation: Pie chart displays proportions as parts of a whole.

questions.question: Which of the following is true about a cumulative frequency graph?

options.b: It is used to calculate median and percentiles

questions.explanation: Cumulative frequency graphs (ogives) are used to estimate median, quartiles, and percentiles.

questions.question: A histogram is used to display:
questions.explanation: Histograms represent continuous or interval data using connected bars.

questions.question: A bar chart is suitable for displaying:
questions.explanation: Bar charts represent frequencies of discrete or categorical variables.

questions.question: A frequency polygon is:

options.b: A line graph connecting class midpoints

questions.explanation: A frequency polygon is a line graph connecting the midpoints of each class interval.

questions.question: Which graph is suitable for showing trends over time?
questions.explanation: Line graphs show trends and changes over time.

questions.question: A stem-and-leaf plot is used to:

options.a: Show data distribution while keeping actual data

questions.explanation: Stem-and-leaf plots display data while preserving actual values.

questions.question: The mean of the data set 2, 4, 6, 8, 10 is:
The mode of 3, 5, 3, 8, 3, 9 is:
questions.explanation: Mode = value occurring most frequently = 3

questions.question: The median of 1, 4, 7, 10, 12, 15 is:
questions.explanation: Median = average of middle two numbers: (7+10)/2 = 17/2 = 8.5

questions.question: Which measure of central tendency is most affected by extreme values?
questions.explanation: Mean is sensitive to outliers, unlike median or mode.

questions.question: The mean of 4, 8, 6, 10, 12 is:
The median of 3, 7, 9, 12, 15, 18 is:
questions.explanation: Median = average of middle two numbers: (9+12)/2 = 21/2 = 10.5

questions.question: The mode of 5, 7, 5, 8, 5, 10 is:
questions.explanation: Mode = most frequent value = 5

questions.question: In a data set, the value that occurs most frequently is called:
questions.explanation: Mode = most frequently occurring value in a dataset.

questions.question: The mean of 10, 15, 20, 25, 30 is:
The median of 5, 8, 12, 14, 20, 25 is:
questions.explanation: Median = average of middle two numbers: (12+14)/2 = 26/2 = 13

questions.question: The mode of 2, 3, 5, 3, 7, 3, 8 is:
questions.explanation: Mode = most frequent value = 3

questions.question: The mean of 4, 8, 6, 10, 12 is:
The median of 7, 3, 12, 10, 5 is:
questions.explanation: Arrange in order: 3, 5, 7, 10, 12 → middle value = 7

questions.question: The mode of 2, 4, 4, 5, 6, 4, 7 is:
questions.explanation: Mode = value occurring most frequently = 4

questions.question: In a dataset, which measure is least affected by outliers?
questions.explanation: Median is robust to extreme values, unlike mean.

questions.question: The range of 5, 9, 12, 4, 8 is:
questions.explanation: Range = Max − Min = 12 − 5 = 7

questions.question: The standard deviation is a measure of:
questions.explanation: Standard deviation measures how spread out the data is around the mean.

questions.question: The variance of 2, 4, 6, 8, 10 is:
questions.explanation: Mean = 6; deviations: -4, -2, 0, 2, 4 → squared = 16,4,0,4,16 → sum = 40 → variance = 40/5 = 8

questions.question: If Q1 = 20 and Q3 = 50, the interquartile range (IQR) is:
Which of the following is NOT a measure of dispersion?
questions.explanation: Mean is a measure of central tendency, not dispersion.

questions.question: If σ² = 25, then the standard deviation σ is:
questions.explanation: σ = √variance → √25 = 5

questions.question: The variance of 2, 4, 6, 8, 10 using the population formula is:
questions.explanation: Mean = 6; deviations: -4, -2, 0, 2, 4 → squared = 16,4,0,4,16 → sum = 40 → variance = 40/5 = 8

questions.question: The difference between the highest and lowest value in a data set is called:
questions.explanation: Range = Max − Min

questions.question: The standard deviation of a data set tells us:

options.b: How spread out the data is from the mean

questions.explanation: Standard deviation measures dispersion around the mean.

questions.question: The interquartile range (IQR) is calculated as:
questions.explanation: IQR = Q3 − Q1, showing the spread of the middle 50% of the data.

questions.question: The range of the data 12, 15, 18, 20, 25 is:
questions.explanation: Range = Max − Min = 25 − 12 = 13

questions.question: Standard deviation is a measure of:
questions.explanation: Standard deviation measures spread of data around the mean.

questions.question: Range is calculated as:
questions.explanation: Range = difference between maximum and minimum values

questions.question: Variance of 3, 5, 7, 9, 11 is:
questions.explanation: Mean = 7; deviations: -4, -2, 0, 2, 4 → squared = 16,4,0,4,16 → sum = 40 → variance = 40/5 = 8

questions.question: Measures of dispersion include:
questions.explanation: Dispersion measures spread of data (variance, SD, range).

questions.question: Cumulative frequency is used to:

options.b: Show total number of observations up to a point

questions.explanation: Cumulative frequency = sum of frequencies up to a certain class or value.

questions.question: In a frequency distribution, cumulative frequency for a class is:

options.b: Sum of frequencies of that and previous classes

questions.explanation: Cumulative frequency = sum of frequencies up to that class

questions.question: The first quartile (Q1) is:
questions.explanation: Q1 = 25th percentile of the data

questions.question: The first quartile (Q1) represents:
questions.explanation: Q1 = 25th percentile, value below which 25% of data lies.

questions.question: The third quartile (Q3) represents:
questions.explanation: Q3 = 75th percentile, value below which 75% of data lies.

questions.question: If a data set is skewed to the right, which is true?
questions.explanation: Right-skewed distribution → mean > median > mode.

questions.question: In a right-skewed distribution, the order of mean, median, and mode is usually:
questions.explanation: Right-skewed → mean > median > mode

questions.question: A right-skewed distribution has:
questions.explanation: Right-skewed → tail on the right → mean > median > mode

questions.question: The sum of deviations of all values from the mean is always:
questions.explanation: Σ(x − x̄) = 0 by definition of the mean.

questions.question: The sum of deviations from the mean in any data set is always:
questions.explanation: By definition, Σ(x − x̄) = 0

questions.question: The sum of deviations of all observations from the mean is always:
questions.explanation: Σ(x − x̄) = 0 by definition of mean

questions.question: Probability of an event always lies between:
questions.explanation: Probability (P) satisfies 0 ≤ P ≤ 1.

questions.question: Probability of an impossible event is:
questions.explanation: Impossible event → P = 0

questions.question: The sum of all probabilities of all possible outcomes in a sample space is:
questions.explanation: ΣP(all events) = 1 by definition of probability.

questions.question: The probability of an event happening is 0.8. The probability of it not happening is:
In a probability experiment, the sum of probabilities of all possible outcomes is:
questions.explanation: By definition, ΣP(all outcomes) = 1

questions.question: Probability of an impossible event is:
questions.explanation: Impossible event → P = 0

questions.question: If P(E) = 0.6, then P(not E) =
Probability of an event is 0.25. Probability of its complement is:
A binomial distribution requires:
questions.explanation: Binomial distribution = fixed number of independent trials with two possible outcomes per trial.

questions.question: The probability of rolling a 4 on a fair die is:
questions.explanation: A die has 6 sides → P(4) = 1/6

questions.question: If mean = 50 and standard deviation = 5, a data value of 60 has a z-score of:
In a normal distribution, mean = 50 and SD = 5. Approximately 68% of data lies between:
questions.explanation: 68% of data lies within 1 SD of the mean → 50 ± 5 = 45 to 55
